author: barnabasbusa
content:
```yaml
zkboost_params:
  image: ghcr.io/eth-act/zkboost/zkboost:0.5.0
  env:
    RUST_LOG: info,zkboost=debug
  instances:
    - name: zkboost-1
      el_participant_index: 0
  zkvms:
    - kind: ere
      proof_type: reth-zisk
      image: ghcr.io/eth-act/ere/ere-server-zisk:0.6.1-cuda
      program_url: https://github.com/eth-act/ere-guests/releases/download/v0.7.0/stateless-validator-reth-zisk
      port: 3000
      gpu:
        device_ids: ["0"]
        shm_size: 32768
        ulimits:
          memlock: -1
      env:
        RUST_LOG: info
        ERE_ZISK_SETUP_ON_INIT: "1"
        ERE_ZISK_START_SERVER_TIMEOUT_SEC: "600"

```

author: barnabasbusa

author: barnabasbusa
content: but I shouldn't need to do any of this

author: barnabasbusa
content:
minimum config should be:

```yaml
participants:
  - cl_type: lighthouse
    cl_image: ethpandaops/lighthouse:eth-act-optional-proofs
    el_type: reth
    el_image: ghcr.io/paradigmxyz/reth
    supernode: true
    cl_extra_params:
      - --target-peers=3
      - --proof-engine-endpoint=http://zkboost-1:3000
      - --proof-types=6
    vc_extra_params:
      - --proof-engine-endpoint=http://zkboost-1:3000
      - --proof-types=6
    count: 2
  - cl_type: lighthouse
    cl_image: ethpandaops/lighthouse:unstable
    el_type: reth
    el_image: ghcr.io/paradigmxyz/reth
    supernode: true
    count: 2

network_params:
  seconds_per_slot: 30

additional_services:
  - zkboost
```

author: han0110
content: currently the mock proof generated are all valid, if we'd want invalid mock proof, we can just set random bytes and the zkboost verify endpoint will return invalid (a valid mock proof still has the right public output)

author: taulepton_
content: why?

author: barnabasbusa
content: Maybe I misunderstand what mock proof is

author: taulepton_
content: The idea of dummy proofs is we want the network to be able to progress without running a GPU prover

author: barnabasbusa
content: mock proof to me sounds like its a random set of strings that just auto approved basically

author: taulepton_
content: Yes basically

author: taulepton_
content: I think that description is correct

author: taulepton_
content: The validity of the proof is defined in the proof node context. The proof node can be spun up in mock mode or real mode. Currently mock mode just defined everything as valid.

author: taulepton_
content: proof node = zkboost

author: han0110
content:
The mock mode still checks the public output and length of the mock proof (as a sanity check), so it's not actually everything as valid, but the idea is correct.

I can change the behavior if this is confusing or not easy to work with in the testing env

author: taulepton_
content: what proof length conditions do you use?

content: makes sense

content: Sure will try to implement this

author: barnabasbusa
content: I'm still trying to find the balance between having something light and easy to run, and something thats actually doing the proofs

author: han0110
content: There is a `mock_proof_size` in the zkboost config you can set for each mock zkvm, it checks if the length is as it specifies

```
    cl_image: ethpandaops/lighthouse:eth-act-optional-proofs
    el_type: reth
    el_image: ghcr.io/paradigmxyz/reth
    supernode: true
    cl_extra_params:
      - --target-peers=3
      - --proof-engine-endpoint=http://zkboost-1:3000
      - --proof-types=6
    vc_extra_params:
      - --proof-engine-endpoint=http://zkboost-1:3000
      - --proof-types=6
    count: 2
  - cl_type: lighthouse
    cl_image: ethpandaops/lighthouse:unstable
    el_type: dummy
    supernode: false
    cl_extra_params:
      - --target-peers=3
      - --proof-engine-endpoint=http://zkboost-1:3000
      - --proof-types=6
    count: 2

```

```
      --proof-types <PROOF-TYPES>
          Comma-separated list of EIP-8025 proof type identifiers (u8) to
          request from peers via ExecutionProofsByRoot and
          ExecutionProofsByRange RPC. If not specified, defaults to '0,1,2,3'
          (EthrexRisc0, EthrexSP1, EthrexZisk, RethOpenVM).
```

author: manunlp
content:
Prysm currently follows the [main branch of the spec](https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/blob/master/specs/_features/eip8025/beacon-chain.md#modified-process_execution_payload). Meaning that the CL needs both an execution engine and a proof engine when running with the `--enable-zkvm` flag.

==> It won't work with `el_type: None`

author: barnabasbusa
content: hm feels redundant?

author: barnabasbusa
content: feels like the execution engine should be optional

author: manunlp
content:
That's a question that was multiple times debated actually in different discord threads.
To the best of my knowledge (and the current state of the spec), it has been decided to keep both, at least, at the moment.

author: barnabasbusa
content: otherwise its very hard to verify that our setup is working or not lol

author: barnabasbusa
content: why couldn't the execution engine not become an optional flag?

author: barnabasbusa
content: use proofs if can, else fallback if defined

author: barnabasbusa
content: whole point of optional proofs

author: barnabasbusa
content: if you make it mandatory to have an engine we never gonna see proofs take off lol

author: manunlp
content: Can't tell for LH, but if you use prysm with the `--enabled-zkvm` flag without at least one prover on the network, the node will consider all blocks as optimistic and thus won't propose any block by itself

author: manunlp
content: (more context of the execution engine + proof engine decision)

author: taulepton_
content:
We plan to keep the EL for optional proofs. The idea is to gather stats and build confidence. We will likely run some with no EL as well to gather stats however the recommendation for the validators on the network will be to run an EL. 

The hope is that by the time mandatory proofs arrives we have the confidence to change the recommendation to no EL.

author: taulepton_
content: I do also agree that support no EL is good for testing but we can defer that for prysm to a later devnet if they chose to support no EL.
